concentration.headline: Highly distributed urban network
concentration.body: No single city overwhelms Spain's urban landscape. Madrid holds 13.4% of tracked urban population across 783 cities — characteristic of federal or historically polycentric countries with multiple strong economic centers.
concentration.comparison: Similar to: United States, Germany, Switzerland

gravity.headline: Linear city corridor — Madrid to Barcelona
gravity.body: The strongest gravitational pull exists between Madrid and Barcelona (506 km apart, combined population 11,011,000). Cities are connected along a corridor averaging 318 km between top pairs — typical of coastal, river valley, or industrial belt development.

narration.synthesis: Spain shows a distributed urban system with no dominant primate city (Madrid at 13.4%), with gravitational connections forming a corridor (Madrid → Barcelona, avg 318 km). Urban power is spread across a geographic axis rather than concentrated at a single hub.
